title: Stop and profit rules compare unit prices to total cost basis

evidence.quote: The test uses Amount=10 and Price=1 while the price series moves from 10 to 9.

reasoning: The close-price indicator returns a per-unit market price, but CostBasis is total position value. For a normal entry of 10 units at 100, a current price of 95 is evaluated as 95/1000-1, a roughly 90% loss instead of 5%. The existing stop-loss test masks this by choosing Amount*Price equal to the series start price.
reproduction: Open BUY amount=10 at Price=100, use a series with ClosePrice=95, and NewStopLossRule(series, -0.1).IsSatisfied returns true even though the unit loss is only 5%.
recommendation: Either compare current unit price to the entry execution unit price, or compare current position value (current unit price * amount) to total cost basis. Apply the same convention to fixed profit.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The stop-loss test does not use a realistic amount/entry-price pair where entry Price equals the series entry price and Amount is greater than one. There is no fixed-profit regression test in the included files.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add stop-loss and fixed-profit cases for Amount=10, entry Price=100, current ClosePrice=95/105, proving thresholds are computed from the intended unit or position-value basis.
